pretest.question: Amy colored 4/6 of a rectangle. How many parts did she NOT color?
hints: Think about what fractions of a whole means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how fractions of a whole works.

A.note: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how fractions of a whole works.

C.note: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how fractions of a whole works.

D.note: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how fractions of a whole works.

pretest.question: A chocolate bar has 3 equal sections. What fraction is each section?

pretest.question: A hexagon is divided into 6 equal parts. 1 part is colored. What fraction is colored?
hints: Count the total equal parts: 6. That is the denominator.

feedback.incorrect: 1 colored part out of 6 equal parts = 1/6.

A.note: 1 colored part out of 6 equal parts = 1/6.

C.note: 1 colored part out of 6 equal parts = 1/6.

D.note: 1 colored part out of 6 equal parts = 1/6.

pretest.question: A strip of paper is folded into 2 equal parts. What unit fraction is each part?

feedback.correct: Right! 2 equal parts → each part is 1/2.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson — U8_understand_fractions/L3_unit_fractions_of_a_whole
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.feedback_correct: Right! 2 equal parts → each part is 1/2.

pretest.question: 6 friends share 1 watermelon equally. What fraction does each friend get?
hints: There are 6 friends sharing equally — the denominator is 6.
1 watermelon divided equally among 6 = 1/6 per friend.

feedback.correct: Right! 6 equal shares → each friend gets 1/6.
feedback.incorrect: Divide 1 watermelon equally among 6 friends → each gets 1/6.

A.note: Divide 1 watermelon equally among 6 friends → each gets 1/6.

B.note: Divide 1 watermelon equally among 6 friends → each gets 1/6.

D.note: Divide 1 watermelon equally among 6 friends → each gets 1/6.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.8 Lesson — U8_understand_fractions/L2_equal_shares
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.feedback_correct: Right! 6 equal shares → each friend gets 1/6.
